items.question: What defines self-supervised learning?
options: Learning from unlabeled data using pseudo-labels derived from data structure
items.answer: Learning from unlabeled data using pseudo-labels derived from data structure
items.explanation: SSL constructs pretext tasks so targets come from the data itself — masked tokens, next tokens, rotation classes, etc.

items.question: In masked language modeling, the pseudo-label for a masked position is:
options: The original token that was masked
The next sentence in the document
items.answer: The original token that was masked
items.explanation: The label already exists in the raw text; masking hides it from the input during training.

items.question: Which is a text self-supervised pretext task?
items.explanation: The following token in the same document serves as the pseudo-label at each position.

items.question: Self-supervised learning vs classic unsupervised clustering — key difference?
options: SSL always trains with a prediction loss on pseudo-labels from pretext tasks
items.answer: SSL always trains with a prediction loss on pseudo-labels from pretext tasks
items.explanation: Both may use unlabeled inputs, but SSL frames an explicit surrogate prediction objective.

items.question: Why do foundation models use self-supervised pretraining?
options: Human labels are available for every web page
Pretext tasks scale to massive unlabeled data and learn transferable representations
Supervised training is always more accurate at scale
items.answer: Pretext tasks scale to massive unlabeled data and learn transferable representations
items.explanation: Web-scale text and images lack human labels; SSL learns structure first, then adapts downstream.
